data.name: Senior Product Manager
data.jobDescription:
Overview

A New Era of Enterprise Commerce Has Begun — And You Are Invited to Help Build It at Microsoft.
Every organization on the planet is shifting to an AI-powered world. How they discover, buy, optimize, and scale Microsoft Cloud must evolve with them. This time, it’s not just people interacting with our systems. It’s AI agents. Autonomous workflows. Self-governing commerce ecosystems. A fundamental reinvention of how cloud value is created and consumed.
At Enterprise Commerce (EC), Microsoft, we are building the platform that will define this future — a commerce engine that is:

  • Planet-scale in reach
  • Agent-driven at its core
  • Intelligent in every workflow
  • Modern, resilient, and radically simplified

The problems we’re solving are bold and consequential.

  • Architecting commerce to be AI-native — where agents purchase, renew, and optimize
  • Modernizing decades of licensing and pricing logic into real-time, predictive, adaptive systems
  • Powering $150B+ of global commerce with next-level reliability and intelligence
  • Building APIs and platforms that every Microsoft Cloud product relies on to serve the world

This isn’t a rewrite of code; it’s a rewrite of how the world engages with Microsoft Cloud. And the team shaping that future is looking for talent like you.

If you are a product manager who is customer obsessed, loves complexity & scale, wants your work to impact every customer touching Azure, M365, Dynamics, Security, and Industry Clouds and is being part of this transformation — this is your arena.



Responsibilities

    • Own product strategy and roadmap for a commerce workflow or platform capability; define outcomes, milestones, and measurable success metrics. 

    • Identify where AI meaningfully improves customer and business outcomes (and where deterministic solutions are better) considering cost, reliability, latency, security, and compliance. 

    • Lead product discovery: customer interviews, problem framing, rapid prototyping, and validation; translate learnings into prioritized requirements. 

    • Write crisp PRDs and user stories for AI-assisted experiences (e.g., guided purchase, renewal assistance, proactive optimization), including guardrails, fallbacks, and evaluation criteria. 

    • Partner with engineering, design, finance, operations, and go-to-market teams to deliver end-to-end experiences from ideation to launch and iteration. 

    • Use generative AI tools to accelerate PM work (analysis, synthesis, documentation, communication) and model best practices for secure and responsible usage. 

    • Communicate product narrative and trade-offs clearly to executives and stakeholders; influence without authority across complex organizations. 



Qualifications
  • 8+ years of software product & program management experience, including at least 3 years or equivalent experience in building enterprise grade B2B products and services.
  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in computer science or a related field, or equivalent experience and MBA from premium institute is preferred.
  • Ownership, drive, and entrepreneurial mindset to build trust with internal and externals stakeholders.
  • Experience in enterprise commerce, billing, subscription management, or related domains
  • Strong collaboration skills to impact/influence engineering and cross functional teams
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and influencing skills.
  • Builds upon a strong team ethos and embodies company and division values, cultivating a culture based in diversity, inclusion, growth mindset, and customer obsession.
  • Passion for customer satisfaction and launching high-quality products leveraging technology, data and AI.

Preferred Qualifications:  

  • Hands-on use of generative AI tools (e.g., Copilot/ChatGPT/Gemini or equivalents) to improve productivity and quality of PM outputs (docs, analysis, planning, comms). 

  • Working knowledge of LLM capabilities and limitations, prompt patterns, grounding/verification, and basic evaluation concepts (quality, safety, latency, cost). 

  • Experience shaping or launching AI-assisted features (even if you did not build models) such as summarization, search assistance, recommendations, copilots, or workflow automation. 

  • Responsible AI mindset: understanding of privacy boundaries, security, transparency, bias risks, and human-in-the-loop design. 

  • Experience in enterprise commerce, billing, subscription management, pricing/licensing, or adjacent domains. 

 

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, colour, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please send a request via the Accommodation request form. 

Benefits/perks listed below may vary depending on the nature of your employment with Microsoft and the country where you work. 


This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.



Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

blogs.title: Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely ru
blogs.description: As digital sovereignty becomes a strategic requirement, organizations are rethinking how they deploy critical infrastructure and AI capabilities under tighter regulatory expectations and higher risk conditions. Microsoft’s approach to sovereignty is grounded in enabling enterprises, public sectors and regulated industries to participate in the digital economy securely, independently and on their own terms. The Microsoft Sovereign Cloud brings together productivity, security and cloud workloads to span both... The post  Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely running even when completely disconnected   appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .

blogs.title: A milestone achievement in our journey to carbon negative
blogs.description: In 2020, Microsoft announced a moonshot commitment to become carbon negative by 2030 — accelerating work across our company to advance the partnerships and technologies needed to advance sustainability for our businesses, our customers and the world. A key milestone on this journey was our aim to match 100% of our annual global electricity consumption... blogs.description: Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO, posted the below message to employees on Viva Engage this morning. I am excited to share a couple updates in two of our core priorities: security and quality. Hayete Gallot is rejoining Microsoft as Executive Vice President, Security, reporting to me. I’ve also asked Charlie Bell to take on a... The post  Updates in two of our core priorities  appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .

While AI Transformation centered on efficiency and productivity, Frontier Transformation challenges us to do more for humanity by democratizing intelligence to unlock creativity and innovation for... blogs.title: Maia 200: The AI accelerator built for inference
blogs.description: Today, we’re proud to introduce Maia 200, a breakthrough inference accelerator engineered to dramatically improve the economics of AI token generation. Maia 200 is an AI inference powerhouse: an accelerator built on TSMC’s 3nm process with native FP8/FP4 tensor cores, a redesigned memory system with 216GB HBM3e at 7 TB/s and 272MB of on-chip SRAM, plus... The post  Maia 200: The AI accelerator built for inference  appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .
